models.description: Schedules railway maintenance jobs on network arcs over a discrete time horizon to minimize total passenger delay, while honoring alternative-service capacity during event requests. Reimplementation of de Weert et al. (2024).

sets_indices.name: A
sets_indices.meaning: set of arcs a=(i,j), i<j: bidirectional direct lines

sets_indices.name: OD
sets_indices.meaning: origin-destination pairs (o,d) in N x N, o != d

sets_indices.name: A_j
sets_indices.meaning: arcs on which job j must be performed (subset of A)

sets_indices.name: C
sets_indices.meaning: combinations of arcs that cannot be simultaneously unavailable

sets_indices.name: E_t
sets_indices.meaning: event requests at time t, each a set of track segments s (sets of consecutive arcs)

sets_indices.name: R_{odi}
sets_indices.meaning: arcs of the i-th of K shortest/preferred routes for pair (o,d)

parameters.name: K
parameters.meaning: number of alternative route options per OD pair

parameters.name: tau_a
parameters.meaning: minimum interval required between two consecutive jobs on arc a

parameters.name: omega^e_a
parameters.meaning: travel time by train on arc a (Euclidean distance)

parameters.name: omega^j_a
parameters.meaning: travel time by alternative service on arc a (= 1.35 * omega^e_a)

parameters.name: Omega_{od}
parameters.meaning: average (nominal) travel time by train from o to d

parameters.name: phi_{odt}
parameters.meaning: passenger demand for pair (o,d) at time t

parameters.name: beta_{odt}
parameters.meaning: share of passengers travelling in the peak moment of period t for (o,d)

parameters.name: Lambda_{at}
parameters.meaning: capacity of alternative services on arc a at time t

parameters.name: M
parameters.meaning: big-M for unlimited train capacity / constraint deactivation

decision_variables.name: y_{jt}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if job j starts at time t

decision_variables.name: x_{at}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if arc a is available (served by train) at time t

decision_variables.name: h_{odti}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if route option i is used for (o,d) at time t

decision_variables.name: w_{at}
decision_variables.meaning: travel time traversing arc a at time t

decision_variables.name: v_{odt}
decision_variables.meaning: travel time from o to d at time t

objective.expression_plain: minimize total passenger delay (excess travel time over the nominal/average travel time, weighted by demand)

constraints.name: job_scheduled_once
constraints.expression_plain: each job starts exactly once and completes within the horizon (eq. 2)

constraints.name: arc_availability_vs_jobs
constraints.indexed_over: a in A, t in T, j in J_a
constraints.expression_plain: if a job occupies arc a around time t the arc is unavailable (eq. 3)

constraints.name: arc_travel_time_def
constraints.indexed_over: a in A, t in T
constraints.expression_plain: arc travel time is train time if available, else alternative-service time (eq. 4)

constraints.name: arc_availability_balance
constraints.expression_plain: total available time on arc a equals horizon minus total processing time on a (eq. 5)

constraints.name: mutually_exclusive_arc_outage
constraints.indexed_over: c in C, t in T (added only if C nonempty)
constraints.expression_plain: arc combinations in C cannot be simultaneously unavailable (eq. 6)

constraints.name: non_overlapping_jobs
constraints.indexed_over: a in A, t in T
constraints.expression_plain: jobs on the same arc may not overlap, respecting the inter-job interval tau_a (eq. 7)

constraints.name: event_segment_capacity
constraints.indexed_over: t in T, s in E_t (added only if E nonempty)
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{a \in s} \sum_{(o,d)} \sum_{i=1, a \in R_{odi}}^{K} h_{odti} \beta_{odt} \phi_{odt} \leq \Lambda_{st} + M \sum_{a \in s} x_{at}
constraints.expression_plain: peak passenger flow on an event track segment must fit alternative-service capacity unless an arc is train-served (big-M relaxation) (eq. 8)

constraints.name: route_choice_unit
constraints.expression_plain: exactly one of the K route options serves each OD flow at each time (eq. 9)

constraints.name: travel_time_lower_bound
constraints.indexed_over: (o,d) in OD, t in T, i in {1..K}
constraints.expression_plain: OD travel time bounded below by chosen route's arc-time sum (big-M activated by h) (eq. 10)

constraints.name: travel_time_upper_bound
constraints.indexed_over: (o,d) in OD, t in T, i in {1..K}
constraints.expression_plain: OD travel time bounded above by each route's arc-time sum (eq. 11)

constraints.name: jobless_arc_always_available
constraints.indexed_over: a in A with J_a = empty, t in T
constraints.expression_plain: arcs never used by any job are always train-available (search-space reduction) (eq. 12)

constraints.name: jobless_arc_traveltime
constraints.indexed_over: a in A with J_a = empty, t in T
constraints.expression_plain: travel time on jobless arcs fixed to train time (search-space reduction) (eq. 13)

constraints.name: forced_availability_event_overload
constraints.indexed_over: (a,t) where a lies in an event segment, a in all K routes of some (o,d), and beta*phi > segment capacity (added only if E nonempty)
constraints.expression_plain: force train availability on arcs whose event-time demand exceeds alternative capacity on every route (eq. 14)

constraints.name: never_used_route_elimination
constraints.indexed_over: (o,d,i) in precomputed never_used_routes, t in T
constraints.expression_plain: dominated route options (min time of i exceeds max time of another option) are disabled (eq. 15)

constraints.name: valid_ineq_sousa_wolsey
constraints.indexed_over: a in A, j in J_a, t in T, delta in {2..Delta_max}
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{t' \in Q_j} y_{jt'} + \sum_{j' \in J_a, j' \neq j} \sum_{t' \in Q'_{j'}} y_{j't'} \leq 1
constraints.expression_plain: Sousa-Wolsey single-machine-scheduling valid inequality, added optionally to tighten the LP relaxation (eq. B1)

constraints.name: valid_ineq_pairwise_nonoverlap
constraints.indexed_over: a in A, j,j' in J_a (j!=j'), t in T, t' in (t-pi_{j'}-tau_a, t+pi_j+tau_a)
constraints.expression_plain: pairwise non-overlap valid inequality on a shared arc, added optionally (eq. B2)

instances.name: railway_N{N}_T{T}_J{J}_P{P}_K{K}.json
instances.size: 27 instances; encoded in filename: N in {10,20,40} stations, T in {10,50,100} periods, J in {10,40,80} jobs, P=1000 passengers, K=3 routes

models.notes: Big-M (M) is data-dependent: M = min(sum of all phi_{odt} + 1, 1e5), capped to avoid numerical issues (railway.py:470-476). Constraints 6, 8, 14 are conditionally added only when their underlying sets (C, E) are nonempty. Constraints 12-15 and B1/B2 are search-space reductions / valid inequalities, not part of the core feasibility model. The repo also implements a simulated-annealing meta-heuristic and cutting planes (not part of the MILP itself). Four Jupyter notebooks (notebooks/model0-2.ipynb, model_formulation.ipynb) demonstrate the same model.

extraction_notes: README 'Model Description' (lines 223-484) provides the full mathematical formulation (eqs. 1-15, B1-B2) which maps one-to-one onto gurobipy code in src/railway/railway.py via the inline '(n)' comment tags. Symbols taken from the README; code identifiers recorded in source_symbol. Paper reference (de Weert et al., Computers & Operations Research 165, 2024) confirmed in README. Nothing was inferred beyond the documented model; valid inequalities Q_j/Q'_{j'}/Delta_max helper ranges are defined in railway.py around lines 840-895.
